user_query:
I'm organizing a youth civic-tech meetup in Suva and want to automate prep so we can focus on solutions. Please implement and run a small CLI script that does three things end-to-end: (1) performs Internet search engine queries to collect a few official Fiji government or Parliament items for our topics, (2) downloads a subset of those links while capturing and analyzing command output/errors, and (3) generates concise meeting notes with concrete action items assigned to our participants.

Details and constraints:
- Inputs (already provided):
  - input/topics.json: a JSON file listing our topics. Use the fields as-is to formulate queries.
  - input/participants.csv: names and roles for action-item assignment.

- Search requirements (Internet required):
  - For each topic in input/topics.json, construct two queries that include the topic name and a site filter limiting results to official Fiji sources: one with a *.gov.fj filter and one with a parliament.gov.fj filter. The exact query syntax is up to you, but it must be a real search engine query performed over the Internet (do not hardcode target URLs).
  - Collect up to 3 result items per query from official domains only (skip non-official domains). For each result, capture at least: query, title (if available), url, source_domain, result_rank, and fetched_at (ISO timestamp).
  - Save a log of every query you executed to output/search/queries_log.json and the aggregated results to output/search/results.json. These files must be valid JSON.

- Download and error analysis requirements:
  - For the first 2 unique result URLs per topic (deduplicate across its two queries), attempt to download each to output/downloads/. Determine a safe filename using the topic, result_rank, and the URL extension if present (default to .html if unknown).
  - Invoke the system curl command via a subprocess for each download (use --location, --fail, and show errors). Capture curl’s exit code, stderr, and the size (bytes) of any downloaded file.
  - Produce output/downloads_report.json with an entry per attempted URL: topic, url, saved_path (or null on failure), exit_code, stderr_excerpt (first ~200 chars), and bytes_downloaded (0 on failure).
  - Produce output/errors.log that aggregates non-zero exit codes and their counts, plus one example stderr line per unique error. This file must be human-readable text.

- Meeting notes and action items:
  - Generate output/meeting_notes.md with these sections: (a) Summary (brief purpose and date), (b) Findings per topic (list the titles and local filenames of any successfully downloaded items), (c) Issues encountered (counts of failed downloads and a one-line explanation referencing errors.log), and (d) Action items.
  - Action items: For each successfully downloaded item, create three tasks tied to participant roles as follows:
    - Data role: "Extract key facts from <filename> for <topic>."
    - Comms role: "Draft a 3-sentence plain-language summary for <topic>."
    - Outreach role: "Identify one youth group or community contact to brief on <topic>."
    Assign tasks by cycling through participants of the matching role from input/participants.csv so work is distributed. If a role has no participants, note the unassigned item in the Action items section.

- General:
  - Do not include direct hardcoded target URLs. The search step must be essential.
  - If curl is unavailable or a download fails, ensure the failure is recorded in downloads_report.json and errors.log, and summarized in the meeting notes.
  - All output paths must be created exactly as specified: output/search/queries_log.json, output/search/results.json, output/downloads/ (files), output/downloads_report.json, output/errors.log, and output/meeting_notes.md.

Deliverables: the script (place it at scripts/fiji_civic_fetcher.py), plus all outputs under output/ as described. Please actually run the script so the outputs are populated. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
